response:
Emily collected 24 blueberries. David collected 15 more blueberries than Emily, which means he collected 24 + 15 = 39 blueberries. David also collected three times as many as Sophia, so Sophia collected 39 / 3 = 13 blueberries. Together, they collected 24 + 39 + 13 = 76 blueberries. 

They used their blueberries to make muffins. One batch of muffins required 9 blueberries, so they made 76 / 9 = 8 batches. Each batch sold for $5, so the total money made is 8 * 5 = $40. 

The muffins were sold and they made $40 from the blueberries they collected. 

*** 

The blueberries they collected were used to make muffins and they made money from the sale. 

The money they made was enough to cover the cost of the muffins and they had some left over. 

They made a profit from the muffins and the money they earned was used for other purposes. 

Youth is the result of their efforts and the blueberries they collected.
